root.title: Root
root.summary:
**Root Section Summary:**

The **Root** section of **Claude Code** documentation provides a comprehensive overview of the **AI-powered coding assistant**, covering its **installation, core architecture, customization, deployment, integrations, and administration**. It includes guidance on **workflow automation, security, extensibility (plugins, hooks, and SDKs), platform-specific integrations, and legal/compliance considerations** to optimize productivity, scalability, and secure usage across diverse technical environments.

children.title: Root
children.summary:
**Root Section Summary:**

The **Root** section of **Claude Code** documentation provides a comprehensive overview of the **AI-powered coding assistant**, covering its **installation, core architecture, customization, deployment, integrations, and administration**. It includes guidance on **workflow automation, security, extensibility (plugins, hooks, and SDKs), platform-specific integrations, and legal/compliance considerations** to optimize productivity, scalability, and secure usage across diverse technical environments.

children.title: Getting Started
children.summary:
**Summary for *Getting Started*:**
This section introduces **Claude Code**, an AI-powered coding assistant designed to integrate seamlessly with your workflow via terminal, IDEs, browser, or desktop. It covers **installation options, core features** (e.g., file editing, Git support, and automation), and **basic usage** across platforms, while emphasizing prerequisites like a Claude subscription and agentic workflows for efficient coding and task automation.

children.title: Claude Code overview
children.summary:
**Claude Code Overview Summary:**

Claude Code is an **agentic coding assistant** that integrates with your codebase via terminal, IDEs (VS Code, JetBrains), browser, and desktop app, enabling file editing, command execution, and workflow automation. The page covers **installation methods** (CLI, VS Code extension, desktop app, etc.), **key features** (e.g., inline diffs, Slack integration, CI/CD support), and **next steps** for getting started, with a focus on cross-platform accessibility and subscription requirements.

children.title: Quickstart
children.summary: The **Quickstart** guide provides a step-by-step introduction to using **Claude Code**, an AI-powered coding assistant, covering installation, login, basic workflows (e.g., asking questions, making edits, and using Git), and essential commands. It supports multiple interfaces (CLI, web, VS Code, Slack, etc.) and highlights prerequisites like a Claude subscription and a code project.

children.title: How Claude Code works
children.summary:
**Summary:**
This page explains **Claude Code’s architecture**, an agentic assistant for the terminal that automates tasks like coding, documentation, and CLI operations. It details its **agentic loop** (context-gathering, action-taking, and verification phases), core components (models, tools, and context management), and **best practices** for effective collaboration, including steering, verification, and session control. Extensibility and workflows are also briefly referenced.

children.title: Core Concepts & Architecture
children.summary:
**Core Concepts & Architecture**
This section introduces the foundational principles and extensible architecture of **Claude Code**, covering how to **customize and enhance its capabilities**—such as adding persistent context, reusable workflows, integrations, and automation—while also providing **practical workflow guidance** (e.g., debugging, refactoring) and **best practices** for optimizing performance, session management, and prompt design to maximize efficiency.

children.title: Extend Claude Code
children.summary:
**Summary:**

This page explains how to **extend Claude Code** by adding custom features like **persistent context (CLAUDE.md), reusable workflows (Skills), external integrations (MCP), isolated tasks (Subagents), automation (Hooks), and plugins** to enhance functionality. It guides users in selecting the right tools based on their goals—such as persistent rules, on-demand capabilities, or background automation—while also covering cost implications and feature interactions.

children.title: Common workflows
children.summary: This page outlines **practical development workflows** using Claude Code, covering tasks like **exploring new codebases, debugging, refactoring, testing, and managing sessions**, with step-by-step prompts for each scenario. Key topics include **Plan Mode for safe analysis, session management, and integrating Claude as a utility or verification tool** in workflows. Example prompts illustrate real-world applications.

children.title: Best Practices for Claude Code
children.summary:
**Summary:**
This page outlines **best practices for using Claude Code**, an autonomous coding agent that explores, plans, and implements tasks based on user prompts. Key topics include **context management, prompt design, environment configuration, session management, and advanced features** (e.g., subagents, hooks, and safe autonomy) to optimize productivity while mitigating performance degradation from limited context window capacity.

children.title: Customization & Configuration
children.summary: **Customization & Configuration** covers how to tailor **Claude Code’s AI models, security, UI, and settings** for performance, safety, and workflow efficiency. Topics include optimizing model behavior (e.g., aliases, memory, speed), enforcing granular permissions and sandboxing, personalizing the terminal interface (themes, shortcuts, status bars), and managing configuration scopes, system prompts, and security best practices for scalable deployment.

children.title: Model & Performance Tuning
children.summary: **Model & Performance Tuning** covers techniques to optimize AI model behavior in Claude Code, including **configuring models** (e.g., selecting aliases, adjusting effort/context), **managing memory** (auto memory vs. custom `CLAUDE.md` files for persistent context), and **enhancing response speed** via fast mode (Opus 4.6) for faster but costlier outputs. It also outlines tradeoffs, use cases, and hierarchical memory management for efficiency.

children.title: Model configuration
children.summary: This page explains **model configuration in Claude Code**, covering how to select and manage AI models via **aliases** (e.g., `default`, `opus`, `sonnet`) or full model names, adjust settings like **effort level** and **context window**, and configure models through **command-line arguments, environment variables, or settings files**. Key topics include available models, special behaviors (e.g., `opusplan` mode), and priority-based model switching.

children.title: Manage Claude's memory
children.summary: This page explains **Claude Code’s memory system**, detailing two types—**auto memory** (persistent session context like preferences and patterns) and **CLAUDE.md files** (customizable markdown instructions for rules, preferences, and project-specific guidance). It covers memory types (managed policy, project, user, and auto memory), their locations, purposes, and hierarchical loading rules, along with options for customization (e.g., modular rules, glob patterns) and best practices for efficient memory management.

children.title: Speed up responses with fast mode
children.summary: This page explains **Claude Code’s *fast mode***—a high-speed configuration for the **Opus 4.6 model** that delivers **2.5x faster responses** at a **higher cost per token** ($30/150 MTok vs. standard pricing). It covers how to **toggle fast mode** (via `/fast` command or settings), its **cost tradeoffs**, ideal use cases (e.g., rapid iteration or live debugging), requirements (subscription plans only), and **rate limit handling**, while noting it’s currently in **research preview**.

children.title: Security & Access Control
children.summary: **Security & Access Control** outlines Claude Code’s **permission system and sandboxing mechanisms** to enforce granular, secure tool and resource access. It details how **allow/ask/deny rules** and **mode-based permissions** (e.g., `default`, `plan`) control tool usage (e.g., file access, web requests), while **sandboxing** provides OS-level isolation to restrict filesystem/network interactions and mitigate risks like prompt injection. The section covers configuration, security trade-offs, and best practices for maintaining autonomy and safety.

children.title: Configure permissions
children.summary:
**Summary:**

This page explains **Claude Code’s permission system**, which allows granular control over tool usage (e.g., file access, Bash commands, or web requests) via **allow/ask/deny rules** and **mode-based settings** (e.g., `default`, `plan`, or `delegate`). It covers rule syntax, precedence, tool-specific permissions (e.g., WebFetch, MCP), and how permissions interact with sandboxing and managed settings, with examples for configuration.

children.title: Sandboxing
children.summary:
**Summary:**

Claude Code’s **sandboxing** feature provides **native security isolation** for agent execution by restricting filesystem and network access to predefined boundaries, reducing the need for constant permission prompts. It uses **OS-level enforcement** to enable safer, more autonomous operations while mitigating risks like **prompt injection** and unauthorized data exfiltration. The page covers setup, configuration options, security benefits, limitations, and best practices for implementing sandboxing.

children.title: User Interface Customization
children.summary: **User Interface Customization** covers how to tailor Claude Code’s terminal experience by adjusting themes, line breaks, notifications, and workflows (e.g., Vim Mode), personalizing the bottom status bar with real-time data like Git status or session costs, and configuring keyboard shortcuts via a JSON-based system to streamline interactions with chat, history, and autocomplete features.

children.title: Optimize your terminal setup
children.summary: This page guides users on **optimizing their terminal setup** for Claude Code, covering key customization options like **themes, line breaks (via `\`, Shift+Enter, or custom shortcuts), notifications (iTerm2 alerts or custom hooks), handling large inputs (file-based workflows), and enabling Vim Mode** for enhanced editing. It also includes terminal-specific configurations (e.g., iTerm2 settings) and troubleshooting tips.

children.title: Customize your status line
children.summary: This page explains how to **customize the status line** in Claude Code, a customizable bar at the bottom that runs shell scripts to display real-time data like **context usage, Git status, session costs, or progress bars**. It covers setting up the status line via the `/statusline` command or manual configuration, available data fields, and practical examples for tracking usage, costs, and session details.

children.title: Customize keyboard shortcuts
children.summary:
**Summary:**

This page explains how to **customize keyboard shortcuts** in Claude Code, detailing the structure of the JSON-based configuration file (`~/.claude/keybindings.json`), available **contexts** (e.g., Chat, Settings, Global), and **actions** (e.g., chat, history, autocomplete) tied to keystrokes. It also covers modifiers, chords, reserved shortcuts, and how to unbind defaults while avoiding terminal conflicts.

children.title: Claude Code settings
children.summary: The **Claude Code settings** page outlines the **configuration system** for customizing Claude Code’s behavior, including **scopes** (Managed, User, Project, Local) to control where settings apply and who they affect. It covers key topics like **permissions, sandboxing, model/configuration, plugins, hooks, and environment variables**, along with guidance on **settings precedence, system prompts, and security best practices** for personal, team, or enterprise use.

children.title: Development & Extensibility
children.summary: **Development & Extensibility** covers how to **customize and extend Claude Code’s functionality** by creating and managing **custom subagents, skills, and plugins**, integrating third-party tools, automating workflows with hooks, and programmatically controlling Claude via the Agent SDK. It also details how to **discover, install, and distribute prebuilt plugins** through marketplaces, as well as build and host your own plugin repositories.

children.title: Create custom subagents
children.summary: This page guides users on **creating custom subagents** in Claude Code, detailing how to design specialized AI assistants for specific tasks. Key topics include setting up subagents with custom system prompts, tools, permissions, and models, configuring their scope and behavior (e.g., foreground/background execution, parallel operations, or chaining), and managing context, delegation rules, and cost efficiency. Example subagents (e.g., code reviewers, debuggers) and advanced features like hooks and persistent memory are also covered.

children.title: Extend Claude with skills
children.summary: This page explains how to **extend Claude with custom skills** by creating `SKILL.md` files that define new capabilities, which Claude can automatically use or invoke via `/skill-name`. Key topics include creating skills, configuring them with frontmatter, managing access and arguments, and advanced patterns like dynamic context injection and subagent execution. The guide also covers sharing, troubleshooting, and aligning with the open **Agent Skills** standard.

children.title: Create plugins
children.summary: This page guides users through **creating custom plugins for Claude Code**, explaining when to use plugins (for shared, reusable functionality) versus standalone configurations (for personal or project-specific tweaks). It covers the plugin structure, development process (including skills, LSP servers, and hooks), testing, debugging, and sharing, with a quickstart section to build a basic plugin. The guide also details migration from standalone configurations to plugins and provides resources for both plugin users and developers.

children.title: Discover and install prebuilt plugins through marketplaces
children.summary: This page explains how to **discover and install prebuilt plugins** for Claude Code via plugin marketplaces, covering marketplace functionality, the official Anthropic marketplace, and different plugin categories like **code intelligence** (e.g., LSP support) and **external integrations**. It also details steps for adding marketplaces (from GitHub, local paths, or URLs), installing plugins, managing them via CLI or interactive interface, and troubleshooting common issues.

children.title: Automate workflows with hooks
children.summary: This page explains **how to automate workflows in Claude Code using hooks**, which are custom shell commands triggered at specific lifecycle stages (e.g., input prompts, code edits, or file protections). It covers setup (e.g., creating a notification hook for user input), key use cases (like auto-formatting code or blocking edits), and advanced topics like structured JSON output, matchers, and troubleshooting common issues. The guide also contrasts deterministic hooks with judgment-based alternatives like prompt- or agent-based hooks.

children.title: Run Claude Code programmatically
children.summary: This page explains how to **run Claude Code programmatically** using the **Agent SDK**, available via **CLI, Python, or TypeScript**, with a focus on the CLI (`claude -p` flag). It covers **basic usage**, **structured output** (JSON/text/streaming), **tool approvals**, **conversation continuation**, and **custom system prompts**, with examples for each feature. The CLI supports non-interactive execution, while the full SDK offers advanced capabilities like hooks and native message handling.

children.title: Create and distribute a plugin marketplace
children.summary: This guide explains how to **create and distribute a custom plugin marketplace** for sharing plugins with teams or communities. It covers defining a `marketplace.json` schema, configuring plugin entries (including Git repositories, local paths, and optional metadata), hosting the marketplace (preferably on GitHub), and troubleshooting common issues like validation errors or authentication failures. The process includes setting up restrictions and testing before distribution.

children.title: Deployment & Infrastructure
children.summary: **Deployment & Infrastructure** covers the **enterprise deployment options, cloud provider integrations, and security configurations** for Claude Code, including self-hosted and managed solutions (e.g., Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry), network and LLM gateway setups for authentication and compliance, and development container best practices for secure, production-ready environments. It provides step-by-step guides for deployment, credential management, and infrastructure customization while addressing security, access controls, and troubleshooting.

children.title: Enterprise deployment overview
children.summary: This page provides an **enterprise deployment overview** for **Claude Code**, outlining key options—**Claude for Teams** (self-service, collaboration-focused) and **Claude for Enterprise** (SSO, compliance, and role-based access)—alongside cloud provider integrations (Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry) for custom infrastructure needs. It compares features like billing, regions, and authentication to help organizations choose the best deployment approach.

children.title: Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
children.summary: This page provides a **step-by-step guide to deploying and configuring Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock**, covering prerequisites (AWS account setup, IAM permissions, and model access), credential configuration (via AWS CLI, environment variables, SSO, or Bedrock API keys), and advanced credential refresh options. It also outlines the process for submitting use case details, configuring Claude Code and model settings, and includes troubleshooting resources.

children.title: Claude Code on Google Vertex AI
children.summary: This page provides a **step-by-step guide to deploying and configuring Claude Code on Google Vertex AI**, covering prerequisites (GCP setup, billing, and model access), region-specific considerations, and detailed setup instructions—including enabling the Vertex AI API, requesting model access, configuring GCP credentials, and setting environment variables for integration. It also touches on IAM configuration, troubleshooting, and additional resources.

children.title: Claude Code on Microsoft Foundry
children.summary: This page provides a **step-by-step guide for deploying and configuring Claude Code with Microsoft Foundry**, covering prerequisites (Azure subscription, RBAC permissions, and Azure CLI), setup (provisioning Foundry resources, Azure credential configuration via API keys or Microsoft Entra ID), and environment variable configurations for enabling integration with Claude’s Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku models. It also touches on Azure RBAC, troubleshooting, and additional resources.

children.title: Enterprise network configuration
children.summary: This page outlines **enterprise network and security configurations** for Claude Code, covering proxy settings (including authentication), custom CA certificates, and **mTLS authentication** via environment variables. It also details **network access requirements** for Claude Code to function, listing critical endpoints like `api.anthropic.com` and `claude.ai`. All configurations can also be applied via `settings.json`.

children.title: LLM gateway configuration
children.summary: This page outlines **LLM gateway configuration** for Claude Code, covering its purpose, requirements, and setup. It details how gateways act as a centralized proxy for authentication, usage tracking, cost controls, and model routing, with specific API format requirements (e.g., Anthropic Messages, Bedrock InvokeModel, Vertex rawPredict). The guide also explains LiteLLM integration, including prerequisites, authentication methods (like static API keys), and basic setup steps for configuring Claude Code with a proxy server.

children.title: Development containers
children.summary: This page explains **Development Containers** for Claude Code, detailing a preconfigured Docker-based environment designed for secure, production-ready Node.js development. Key topics include **core features** (e.g., security-focused firewall, VS Code integration, and session persistence), a **4-step setup guide**, **configuration components** (like `devcontainer.json`, `Dockerfile`, and firewall scripts), and **security considerations** (e.g., isolation risks when bypassing permissions, trusted repository use, and monitoring). Example use cases and team onboarding are also briefly mentioned.

children.title: Platform Integrations
children.summary: **Platform Integrations** covers the various ways **Claude Code** integrates with different environments and tools to enhance AI-powered coding and automation. This includes browser-based solutions (Chrome extension, web assistant), desktop applications (standalone app, JetBrains IDE plugins), IDE-specific integrations (VS Code), version control and CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab), Slack-based coding assistance, and general troubleshooting, customization, and external tool connectivity. The section emphasizes seamless workflow integration, security, and practical use cases across diverse technical platforms.

children.title: Browser-Based Integrations
children.summary: **Browser-Based Integrations** covers AI-powered coding and automation tools designed to enhance productivity within web browsers. It includes **Claude Code’s web-based AI assistant** (with workflows, security policies, and tool integrations) and the **Claude Code Chrome extension (beta)**, which enables live debugging, browser automation, and secure AI-assisted tasks directly in Chrome or Edge. Both solutions emphasize seamless integration with existing workflows while addressing security and limitations.

children.title: Claude Code on the web
children.summary: **Claude Code on the Web** is an AI-powered coding assistant that integrates with web browsers, enabling users to write, debug, and manage code directly in their browser. The page covers its **usage, workflows (e.g., diff view, task teleportation between web/terminal), security policies, supported tools (package managers, databases, cloud platforms), and limitations** for different account types (Enterprise, Max, Pro). It also details **environment setup, network access, and best practices** for secure and efficient coding.

children.title: Use Claude Code with Chrome (beta)
children.summary: The **Claude Code Chrome extension (beta)** enables browser automation and integration with Claude’s AI capabilities directly from the Chrome browser. It allows users to perform tasks like live debugging, web app testing, form automation, and data extraction while leveraging Chrome’s logged-in sessions for secure interactions. Key features include real-time browser execution, session recording, and support for workflows combining coding and browser actions, though currently limited to Chrome and Edge.

children.title: Desktop Applications
children.summary: **Desktop Applications** covers the integration of **Claude Code** with desktop environments, including its **standalone AI-powered coding assistant** (features, customization, and enterprise configurations) and **JetBrains IDE plugins** (supported tools, key functionalities, installation, and security). The section provides setup guidance, feature comparisons with CLI versions, and troubleshooting for both desktop and IDE-specific workflows.

children.title: Claude Code on desktop
children.summary: **Claude Code on desktop** is a preview AI-powered coding assistant that integrates with your codebase via a graphical interface, offering real-time file editing, terminal command execution, and project understanding—similar to its CLI version but with added visual session management. The documentation outlines installation, core features (like the **Code**, **Cowork**, and **Chat** tabs), customization (e.g., permissions, hooks, and external tool connections), troubleshooting, and differences from the CLI. It also covers enterprise configurations and shared configurations between desktop and CLI.

children.title: JetBrains IDEs
children.summary: This page details **JetBrains IDE integration with Claude Code**, highlighting its **supported IDEs (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, Android Studio, etc.)**, **key features** like interactive diff viewing, selection context sharing, and file reference shortcuts, and **installation/usage instructions** via the JetBrains Marketplace or terminal commands (`/ide`). It also covers configuration, troubleshooting, and security considerations.

children.title: Use Claude Code in VS Code
children.summary: This page guides users on **integrating and utilizing Claude Code within Visual Studio Code (VS Code)**, covering installation, core features like prompt editing, file referencing, and conversation management, as well as troubleshooting common issues. It highlights the extension’s capabilities—such as real-time code editing, multi-conversation support, and integration with Git—while also comparing it to the CLI version and addressing security/privacy considerations.

children.title: Version Control & CI/CD Platforms
children.summary: **Version Control & CI/CD Platforms** covers the integration of AI-driven automation tools (**Claude Code**) into **GitHub Actions** and **GitLab CI/CD pipelines**, enabling users to automate code generation, bug fixes, and pull request/merge request workflows. The section details setup, advanced configurations (e.g., model selection, security, and cloud provider integrations like AWS Bedrock or Google Vertex AI), practical use cases, and troubleshooting for seamless AI-assisted development.

children.title: Claude Code GitHub Actions
children.summary: **Claude Code GitHub Actions** integrates AI-driven automation into GitHub workflows, enabling users to generate code changes, fix bugs, and create pull requests via `@claude` commands in PRs/issues. It leverages the **Claude Agent SDK** for custom automation and supports advanced features like model configuration (e.g., **Claude Opus 4.6**), security, and performance optimization. Key topics include setup, use cases, troubleshooting, and integration with AWS Bedrock/Google Vertex AI.

children.title: Claude Code GitLab CI/CD
children.summary: **Claude Code GitLab CI/CD** integrates AI-powered automation into GitLab pipelines, enabling users to generate merge requests (MRs), implement code changes, and resolve issues directly from CI/CD jobs. The guide covers setup (quick or manual), use cases (e.g., turning issues into MRs or fixing bugs), configuration options (including AWS Bedrock/Google Vertex AI), security best practices, and troubleshooting common issues like authentication errors or API limitations.

children.title: Claude Code in Slack
children.summary:
**Summary:**

This page details the **Claude Code integration for Slack**, explaining how to use AI-powered coding assistance directly within Slack conversations. It covers **use cases** (e.g., bug fixes, code reviews), **setup steps**, **access controls**, **session workflows**, and **troubleshooting** for tasks like repository selection and authentication errors. The integration routes coding requests to Claude Code on the web while keeping context within Slack.

children.title: Other
children.summary:
**Summary of "Other":**
This section addresses **troubleshooting common issues** with Claude Code installation, usage, and system integration across Windows, Linux, and Mac; **connecting external tools via MCP** (including setup, security, and examples); and **customizing Claude’s output styles** (built-in options, customization methods, and frontmatter syntax).

children.title: Troubleshooting
children.summary: This **Troubleshooting** page provides solutions for common issues related to **Claude Code installation, setup, and usage**, covering **Windows (WSL), Linux, and Mac systems**. Key topics include **installation errors (e.g., OS detection, Node.js conflicts), authentication/permissions, performance issues (CPU/memory, hangs), IDE integration problems (JetBrains, WSL2), and formatting/Markdown errors**, along with best practices for resolving them.

children.title: Connect Claude Code to tools via MCP
children.summary: This page explains how to **connect Claude Code to external tools via the Model Context Protocol (MCP)**, covering key topics like **installing and managing MCP servers** (HTTP, SSE, or local), **authentication methods**, **scope-based configurations** (local, project, user), **practical examples** (e.g., GitHub, PostgreSQL), and **security controls** (allowlists, denylists, and restrictions). It also includes guidance for server authors, tool search scaling, and resource usage limits.

children.title: Output styles
children.summary: This page explains **Claude Code’s output styles**, which modify the system prompt to tailor Claude’s behavior for different tasks while preserving core capabilities like scripting and file operations. It covers **three built-in styles** (Default, Explanatory, and Learning), **how they work** (via system prompt adjustments), **how to switch or customize styles** (via CLI commands or `.claude/settings.local.json`), and **frontmatter syntax** for creating new styles.

children.title: Administration & Operations
children.summary: **Administration & Operations** covers the **setup, management, and optimization** of the **Claude Code** application, including **system configuration, authentication methods, security protocols, resource/cost management, and team analytics** to ensure secure, efficient, and scalable deployment. It provides guidance on **policy enforcement, monitoring, and productivity tracking** for organizations using Claude Code.

children.title: System Configuration
children.summary:
**System Configuration**
This section outlines the installation, setup, and management of the **Claude Code** application, including **system requirements, installation methods (native, Homebrew, WinGet, etc.), authentication, updates, and security considerations**. It also covers **server-managed settings (public beta)**, a centralized configuration option for organizations to enforce policies via the **Claude.ai admin console** without traditional MDM.

children.title: Set up Claude Code
children.summary: The **Set up Claude Code** page provides a comprehensive guide to installing and configuring the **Claude Code** application, covering **system requirements** (OS, hardware, network, and location), **installation methods** (native, Homebrew, WinGet, and deprecated NPM), **platform-specific setup**, **authentication**, and **update/management options** for both individuals and teams. It also touches on **security, dependencies (e.g., Node.js, ripgrep), and optional cleanup** of configuration files.

children.title: Configure server-managed settings (public beta)
children.summary: This page explains **server-managed settings (public beta)** for **Claude Code**, a centralized configuration method for organizations without MDM or endpoint management. It covers requirements (e.g., plan, client version), how to configure settings via the **Claude.ai admin console**, and key differences from endpoint-managed settings, including security, precedence, and delivery mechanisms. The feature is currently in beta for **Claude for Teams/Enterprise** users.

children.title: Authentication
children.summary: The **Authentication** page outlines how to set up access to **Claude Code** by Anthropic, detailing three primary methods: **Claude for Teams/Enterprise** (recommended for centralized billing and team management), **Claude Console authentication** (for API-based billing with role-based access), and **cloud provider authentication** (via Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, or Microsoft Foundry). It also covers credential management and system requirements for each approach.

children.title: Security
children.summary: The **Security** page for Claude Code outlines its comprehensive security approach, emphasizing a **permission-based architecture** (strict read-only by default, explicit approval for actions) and **built-in protections** like sandboxed bash tools, write-access restrictions, and prompt fatigue mitigation. It also covers user responsibilities, safeguards for privacy, sensitive code handling, and best practices for team and cloud security, while directing users to resources like SOC 2 compliance and reporting security issues.

children.title: Resource & Cost Optimization
children.summary: **Resource & Cost Optimization** covers Claude Code’s **data policies and usage controls** (including training opt-ins, feedback mechanisms, and retention), **monitoring tools** (OpenTelemetry integration for tracking performance, metrics, and costs), and **cost-saving strategies** (tracking spend, setting limits, optimizing API/model usage, and leveraging tools like `/cost` and subagents). The section emphasizes transparency, observability, and proactive management of resource allocation.

children.title: Data usage
children.summary: This **Data Usage** page outlines **Claude Code’s data policies**, including how user data (from Free/Pro/Max accounts) may be used for model training unless disabled, while **commercial users** (Team/Enterprise/API) retain stricter controls unless they opt into programs like the **Development Partner Program**. It also covers **feedback mechanisms** (e.g., `/bug` reports, session surveys), **data retention**, **telemetry**, and **data flow** differences between local and cloud execution.

children.title: Monitoring
children.summary: The **Monitoring** page for Claude Code documentation outlines how to enable and configure **OpenTelemetry (OTel) metrics and events** for monitoring its usage, performance, and costs. It covers **quick-start setup** (via environment variables), **key metrics** (e.g., code edits, API requests, token usage), **events** (e.g., user prompts, API errors), **alerting/segmentation**, and **backend considerations** for observability, while emphasizing user responsibility for backend configuration and aggregation granularity.

children.title: Manage costs effectively
children.summary: This page provides strategies for **tracking, managing, and reducing costs** associated with Claude Code usage, including token consumption, API costs, and team-level spending. Key topics cover tools like the `/cost` command, workspace spend limits, rate limits, model selection, and optimizations like offloading tasks to subagents or plugins to minimize expenses.

children.title: Track team usage with analytics
children.summary:
**Summary:**

This page explains how to use **Claude Code’s analytics dashboards** to monitor developer activity, track contribution metrics (via GitHub integration), and measure productivity impact (e.g., PRs, code acceptance rates) for **Teams/Enterprise** and **API customers**. Key features include usage metrics, leaderboards, data exports, and adoption tracking to optimize engineering workflows.

children.title: Reference Guides
CLI reference
children.summary:
The **CLI reference** page documents the **Claude Code CLI commands and flags** for interacting with the tool, including:
- **Core commands** like running interactive mode (`claude`), querying via SDK (`claude -p`), continuing/resuming sessions (`-c`, `-r`), and updating the CLI (`claude update`).
- **Customization flags** for agents, system prompts, permissions, tools, and debugging, along with advanced features like Chrome integration and beta APIs.

children.title: Orchestrate teams of Claude Code sessions
children.summary: This page explains **Claude Code Agent Teams**, an experimental feature enabling multiple Claude Code instances to collaborate as a team, with one session acting as the lead to coordinate tasks, assign work, and synthesize results. It covers setup (enabling teams, specifying teammates, and task delegation), key differences from subagents, best practices, troubleshooting common issues, and use cases like parallel code reviews or hypothesis-driven investigations.

children.title: Plugins reference
children.summary: The **Plugins reference** page provides a detailed technical guide on the **Claude Code plugin system**, covering **component schemas (skills, agents, hooks, servers), plugin installation, CLI commands, debugging tools, and best practices** for development, distribution, and troubleshooting. It also outlines **directory structures, caching mechanisms, security rules, and versioning** for creating and managing plugins.

children.title: Interactive mode
children.summary: This page details **Claude Code’s Interactive Mode**, covering **keyboard shortcuts, text editing, and advanced features** like **Vim mode, multiline input, background commands, and task management**. It also explains **model switching, permission toggles, and terminal customization** for efficient workflows.

children.title: Checkpointing
children.summary: **Checkpointing in Claude Code** is an automatic feature that saves the state of your code after each edit, allowing you to **undo changes, rewind to prior versions, or summarize conversation history** for better context management. Key functions include **restoring code/conversation, targeted summarization (without altering files), and branching sessions** for experimentation, with limitations on tracking external or CLI changes.

children.title: Legal and compliance
children.summary: This **Legal and Compliance** page outlines the terms, agreements, and policies governing **Claude Code** usage, including **licensing** (Commercial vs. Consumer Terms), **compliance** (e.g., BAA for healthcare), **security and trust** measures, and **vulnerability reporting** via HackerOne. It also references external resources like Anthropic’s **Trust Center** and **Transparency Hub** for further details.

children.title: Hooks reference
children.summary:
This **Hooks reference** page provides a detailed guide on **customizing and extending Claude Code’s functionality** using hooks, covering:
- **Hook lifecycle, resolution, and configuration**, including locations, matcher patterns, and input/output handling (e.g., JSON, exit codes).
- **Event-specific hooks** (e.g., `SessionStart`, `PreToolUse`, `TaskCompleted`) with their inputs, decision controls, and use cases.
- **Advanced topics** like prompt/agent-based hooks, async execution, and examples (e.g., multi-criteria stops, background tasks).
